## plugin:qmd-plugin:qmd
QMD is your local search engine over 34 markdown documents.

Collections (scope with `collection` parameter):
  - "electrobun-python-sidecar-extraction" (8 docs)
  - "my-electrobun-extraction" (5 docs)
  - "my-electrobun-docs" (11 docs)
  - "my-electrobun-temporal" (4 docs)
  - "electrobun-python-sidecar-temporal" (4 docs)
  - "electrobun-python-sidecar-brief" (1 docs)
  - "my-electrobun-brief" (1 docs)

Search: Use `query` with sub-queries (lex/vec/hyde):
  - type:'lex' — BM25 keyword search (exact terms, fast)
  - type:'vec' — semantic vector search (meaning-based)
  - type:'hyde' — hypothetical document (write what the answer looks like)

  Always provide `intent` on every search call to disambiguate and improve snippets.

Examples:
  Quick keyword lookup: [{type:'lex', query:'error handling'}]
  Semantic search: [{type:'vec', query:'how to handle errors gracefully'}]
  Best results: [{type:'lex', query:'error'}, {type:'vec', query:'error handling best practices'}]
  With intent: searches=[{type:'lex', query:'performance'}], intent='web page load times'

Retrieval:
  - `get` — single document by path or docid (#abc123). Supports line offset (`file.md:100`).
  - `multi_get` — batch retrieve by glob (`journals/2025-05*.md`) or comma-separated list.

Tips:
  - File paths in results are relative to their collection.
  - Use `minScore: 0.5` to filter low-confidence results.
  - Results include a `context` field describing the content type.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- remotion-best-practices: Best practices for Remotion - Video creation in React
- ccc: This skill should be used when code search is needed (whether explicitly requested or as part of completing a task), when indexing the codebase after changes, or when the user asks about ccc, cocoindex-code, or the codebase index. Trigger phrases include 'search the codebase', 'find code related to', 'update the index', 'ccc', 'cocoindex-code'.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- ast-grep: Guide for writing ast-grep rules to perform structural code search and analysis. 1	# Dev Story Workflow
2	
3	**Goal:** Execute story implementation following a context filled story spec file.
4	
5	**Your Role:** Developer implementing the story.
6	- Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}
7	- Generate all documents in {document_output_language}
8	- Only modify the story file in these areas: Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record (Debug Log, Completion Notes), File List, Change Log, and Status
9	- Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps
10	- Absolutely DO NOT stop because of "milestones", "significant progress", or "session boundaries". Continue in a single execution until the story is COMPLETE (all ACs satisfied and all tasks/subtasks checked) UNLESS a HALT condition is triggered or the USER gives other instruction.
11	- Do NOT schedule a "next session" or request review pauses unless a HALT condition applies. Only Step 6 decides completion.
12	- User skill level ({user_skill_level}) affects conversation style ONLY, not code updates.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## INITIALIZATION
17	
18	### Configuration Loading
19	
20	Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml` and resolve:
21	
22	- `project_name`, `user_name`
23	- `communication_language`, `document_output_language`
24	- `user_skill_level`
25	- `implementation_artifacts`
26	- `date` as system-generated current datetime
27	
28	### Paths
29	
30	- `story_file` = `` (explicit story path; auto-discovered if empty)
31	- `sprint_status` = `{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml`
32	
33	### Context
34	
35	- `project_context` = `**/project-context.md` (load if exists)
36	
37	---
38	
39	## EXECUTION
40	
41	<workflow>
42	  <critical>Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}</critical>
43	  <critical>Generate all documents in {document_output_language}</critical>
44	  <critical>Only modify the story file in these areas: Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record (Debug Log, Completion Notes), File List,
45	    Change Log, and Status</critical>
46	  <critical>Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps</critical>
47	  <critical>Absolutely DO NOT stop because of "milestones", "significant progress", or "session boundaries". Continue in a single execution
48	    until the story is COMPLETE (all ACs satisfied and all tasks/subtasks checked) UNLESS a HALT condition is triggered or the USER gives
49	    other instruction.</critical>
50	  <critical>Do NOT schedule a "next session" or request review pauses unless a HALT condition applies. Only Step 6 decides completion.</critical>
51	  <critical>User skill level ({user_skill_level}) affects conversation style ONLY, not code updates.</critical>
52	
53	  <step n="1" goal="Find next ready story and load it" tag="sprint-status">
54	    <check if="{{story_path}} is provided">
55	      <action>Use {{story_path}} directly</action>
56	      <action>Read COMPLETE story file</action>
57	      <action>Extract story_key from filename or metadata</action>
58	      <goto anchor="task_check" />
59	    </check>
60

# Dev Story Workflow

**Goal:** Execute story implementation following a context filled story spec file.

**Your Role:** Developer implementing the story.
- Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}
- Generate all documents in {document_output_language}
- Only modify the story file in these areas: Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record (Debug Log, Completion Notes), File List, Change Log, and Status
- Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps
- Absolutely DO NOT stop because of "milestones", "significant progress", or "session boundaries". Continue in a single execution until the story is COMPLETE (all ACs satisfied and all tasks/subtasks checked) UNLESS a HALT condition is triggered or the USER gives other instruction.
- Do NOT schedule a "next session" or request review pauses unless a HALT condition applies. Only Step 6 decides completion.
- User skill level ({user_skill_level}) affects conversation style ONLY, not code updates.

---

## INITIALIZATION

### Configuration Loading

Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml` and resolve:

- `project_name`, `user_name`
- `communication_language`, `document_output_language`
- `user_skill_level`
- `implementation_artifacts`
- `date` as system-generated current datetime

### Paths

- `story_file` = `` (explicit story path; auto-discovered if empty)
- `sprint_status` = `{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml`

### Context

- `project_context` = `**/project-context.md` (load if exists)

---

## EXECUTION

<workflow>
  <critical>Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}</critical>
  <critical>Generate all documents in {document_output_language}</critical>
  <critical>Only modify the story file in these areas: Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record (Debug Log, Completion Notes), File List,
    Change Log, and Status</critical>
  <critical>Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps</critical>
  <critical>Absolutely DO NOT stop because of "milestones", "significant progress", or "session boundaries". Continue in a single execution
    until the story is COMPLETE (all ACs satisfied and all tasks/subtasks checked) UNLESS a HALT condition is triggered or the USER gives
    other instruction.</critical>
  <critical>Do NOT schedule a "next session" or request review pauses unless a HALT condition applies. Only Step 6 decides completion.</critical>
  <critical>User skill level ({user_skill_level}) affects conversation style ONLY, not code updates.</critical>

  <step n="1" goal="Find next ready story and load it" tag="sprint-status">
    <check if="{{story_path}} is provided">
      <action>Use {{story_path}} directly</action>
      <action>Read COMPLETE story file</action>
      <action>Extract story_key from filename or metadata</action>
      <goto anchor="task_check" />
    </check>

    <!-- Sprint-based story discovery -->
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file exists">
      <critical>MUST read COMPLETE sprint-status.yaml file from start to end to preserve order</critical>
      <action>Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}</action>
      <action>Read ALL lines from beginning to end - do not skip any content</action>
      <action>Parse the development_status section completely to understand story order</action>

      <action>Find the FIRST story (by reading in order from top to bottom) where:
        - Key matches pattern: number-number-name (e.g., "1-2-user-auth")
        - NOT an epic key (epic-X) or retrospective (epic-X-retrospective)
        - Status value equals "ready-for-dev"
      </action>

      <check if="no ready-for-dev or in-progress story found">
        <output>📋 No ready-for-dev stories found in sprint-status.yaml

          **Current Sprint Status:** {{sprint_status_summary}}

          **What would you like to do?**
          1. Run `create-story` to create next story from epics with comprehensive context
          2. Run `*validate-create-story` to improve existing stories before development (recommended quality check)
          3. Specify a particular story file to develop (provide full path)
          4. Check {{sprint_status}} file to see current sprint status

          💡 **Tip:** Stories in `ready-for-dev` may not have been validated. Consider running `validate-create-story` first for a quality
          check.
        </output>
        <ask>Choose option [1], [2], [3], or [4], or specify story file path:</ask>

        <check if="user chooses '1'">
          <action>HALT - Run create-story to create next story</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '2'">
          <action>HALT - Run validate-create-story to improve existing stories</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '3'">
          <ask>Provide the story file path to develop:</ask>
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <goto anchor="task_check" />
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '4'">
          <output>Loading {{sprint_status}} for detailed status review...</output>
          <action>Display detailed sprint status analysis</action>
          <action>HALT - User can review sprint status and provide story path</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user provides story file path">
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <goto anchor="task_check" />
        </check>
      </check>
    </check>

    <!-- Non-sprint story discovery -->
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file does NOT exist">
      <action>Search {implementation_artifacts} for stories directly</action>
      <action>Find stories with "ready-for-dev" status in files</action>
      <action>Look for story files matching pattern: *-*-*.md</action>
      <action>Read each candidate story file to check Status section</action>

      <check if="no ready-for-dev stories found in story files">
        <output>📋 No ready-for-dev stories found

          **Available Options:**
          1. Run `create-story` to create next story from epics with comprehensive context
          2. Run `*validate-create-story` to improve existing stories
          3. Specify which story to develop
        </output>
        <ask>What would you like to do? Choose option [1], [2], or [3]:</ask>

        <check if="user chooses '1'">
          <action>HALT - Run create-story to create next story</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '2'">
          <action>HALT - Run validate-create-story to improve existing stories</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '3'">
          <ask>It's unclear what story you want developed. Please provide the full path to the story file:</ask>
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <action>Continue with provided story file</action>
        </check>
      </check>

      <check if="ready-for-dev story found in files">
        <action>Use discovered story file and extract story_key</action>
      </check>
    </check>

    <action>Store the found story_key (e.g., "1-2-user-authentication") for later status updates</action>
    <action>Find matching story file in {implementation_artifacts} using story_key pattern: {{story_key}}.md</action>
    <action>Read COMPLETE story file from discovered path</action>

    <anchor id="task_check" />

    <action>Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status</action>

    <action>Load comprehensive context from story file's Dev Notes section</action>
    <action>Extract developer guidance from Dev Notes: architecture requirements, previous learnings, technical specifications</action>
    <action>Use enhanced story context to inform implementation decisions and approaches</action>

    <action>Identify first incomplete task (unchecked [ ]) in Tasks/Subtasks</action>

    <action if="no incomplete tasks">
      <goto step="6">Completion sequence</goto>
    </action>
    <action if="story file inaccessible">HALT: "Cannot develop story without access to story file"</action>
    <action if="incomplete task or subtask requirements ambiguous">ASK user to clarify or HALT</action>
  </step>

  <step n="2" goal="Load project context and story information">
    <critical>Load all available context to inform implementation</critical>

    <action>Load {project_context} for coding standards and project-wide patterns (if exists)</action>
    <action>Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status</action>
    <action>Load comprehensive context from story file's Dev Notes section</action>
    <action>Extract developer guidance from Dev Notes: architecture requirements, previous learnings, technical specifications</action>
    <action>Use enhanced story context to inform implementation decisions and approaches</action>
    <output>✅ **Context Loaded**
      Story and project context available for implementation
    </output>
  </step>

  <step n="3" goal="Detect review continuation and extract review context">
    <critical>Determine if this is a fresh start or continuation after code review</critical>

    <action>Check if "Senior Developer Review (AI)" section exists in the story file</action>
    <action>Check if "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection exists under Tasks/Subtasks</action>

    <check if="Senior Developer Review section exists">
      <action>Set review_continuation = true</action>
      <action>Extract from "Senior Developer Review (AI)" section:
        - Review outcome (Approve/Changes Requested/Blocked)
        - Review date
        - Total action items with checkboxes (count checked vs unchecked)
        - Severity breakdown (High/Med/Low counts)
      </action>
      <action>Count unchecked [ ] review follow-up tasks in "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection</action>
      <action>Store list of unchecked review items as {{pending_review_items}}</action>

      <output>⏯️ **Resuming Story After Code Review** ({{review_date}})

        **Review Outcome:** {{review_outcome}}
        **Action Items:** {{unchecked_review_count}} remaining to address
        **Priorities:** {{high_count}} High, {{med_count}} Medium, {{low_count}} Low

        **Strategy:** Will prioritize review follow-up tasks (marked [AI-Review]) before continuing with regular tasks.
      </output>
    </check>

    <check if="Senior Developer Review section does NOT exist">
      <action>Set review_continuation = false</action>
      <action>Set {{pending_review_items}} = empty</action>

      <output>🚀 **Starting Fresh Implementation**

        Story: {{story_key}}
        Story Status: {{current_status}}
        First incomplete task: {{first_task_description}}
      </output>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="4" goal="Mark story in-progress" tag="sprint-status">
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file exists">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}</action>
      <action>Read all development_status entries to find {{story_key}}</action>
      <action>Get current status value for development_status[{{story_key}}]</action>

      <check if="current status == 'ready-for-dev' OR review_continuation == true">
        <action>Update the story in the sprint status report to = "in-progress"</action>
        <action>Update last_updated field to current date</action>
        <output>🚀 Starting work on story {{story_key}}
          Status updated: ready-for-dev → in-progress
        </output>
      </check>

      <check if="current status == 'in-progress'">
        <output>⏯️ Resuming work on story {{story_key}}
          Story is already marked in-progress
        </output>
      </check>

      <check if="current status is neither ready-for-dev nor in-progress">
        <output>⚠️ Unexpected story status: {{current_status}}
          Expected ready-for-dev or in-progress. Continuing anyway...
        </output>
      </check>

      <action>Store {{current_sprint_status}} for later use</action>
    </check>

    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file does NOT exist">
      <output>ℹ️ No sprint status file exists - story progress will be tracked in story file only</output>
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "no-sprint-tracking"</action>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="5" goal="Implement task following red-green-refactor cycle">
    <critical>FOLLOW THE STORY FILE TASKS/SUBTASKS SEQUENCE EXACTLY AS WRITTEN - NO DEVIATION</critical>

    <action>Review the current task/subtask from the story file - this is your authoritative implementation guide</action>
    <action>Plan implementation following red-green-refactor cycle</action>

    <!-- RED PHASE -->
    <action>Write FAILING tests first for the task/subtask functionality</action>
    <action>Confirm tests fail before implementation - this validates test correctness</action>

    <!-- GREEN PHASE -->
    <action>Implement MINIMAL code to make tests pass</action>
    <action>Run tests to confirm they now pass</action>
    <action>Handle error conditions and edge cases as specified in task/subtask</action>

    <!-- REFACTOR PHASE -->
    <action>Improve code structure while keeping tests green</action>
    <action>Ensure code follows architecture patterns and coding standards from Dev Notes</action>

    <action>Document technical approach and decisions in Dev Agent Record → Implementation Plan</action>

    <action if="new dependencies required beyond story specifications">HALT: "Additional dependencies need user approval"</action>
    <action if="3 consecutive implementation failures occur">HALT and request guidance</action>
    <action if="required configuration is missing">HALT: "Cannot proceed without necessary configuration files"</action>

    <critical>NEVER implement anything not mapped to a specific task/subtask in the story file</critical>
    <critical>NEVER proceed to next task until current task/subtask is complete AND tests pass</critical>
    <critical>Execute continuously without pausing until all tasks/subtasks are complete or explicit HALT condition</critical>
    <critical>Do NOT propose to pause for review until Step 9 completion gates are satisfied</critical>
  </step>

  <step n="6" goal="Author comprehensive tests">
    <action>Create unit tests for business logic and core functionality introduced/changed by the task</action>
    <action>Add integration tests for component interactions specified in story requirements</action>
    <action>Include end-to-end tests for critical user flows when story requirements demand them</action>
    <action>Cover edge cases and error handling scenarios identified in story Dev Notes</action>
  </step>

  <step n="7" goal="Run validations and tests">
    <action>Determine how to run tests for this repo (infer test framework from project structure)</action>
    <action>Run all existing tests to ensure no regressions</action>
    <action>Run the new tests to verify implementation correctness</action>
    <action>Run linting and code quality checks if configured in project</action>
    <action>Validate implementation meets ALL story acceptance criteria; enforce quantitative thresholds explicitly</action>
    <action if="regression tests fail">STOP and fix before continuing - identify breaking changes immediately</action>
    <action if="new tests fail">STOP and fix before continuing - ensure implementation correctness</action>
  </step>

  <step n="8" goal="Validate and mark task complete ONLY when fully done">
    <critical>NEVER mark a task complete unless ALL conditions are met - NO LYING OR CHEATING</critical>

    <!-- VALIDATION GATES -->
    <action>Verify ALL tests for this task/subtask ACTUALLY EXIST and PASS 100%</action>
    <action>Confirm implementation matches EXACTLY what the task/subtask specifies - no extra features</action>
    <action>Validate that ALL acceptance criteria related to this task are satisfied</action>
    <action>Run full test suite to ensure NO regressions introduced</action>

    <!-- REVIEW FOLLOW-UP HANDLING -->
    <check if="task is review follow-up (has [AI-Review] prefix)">
      <action>Extract review item details (severity, description, related AC/file)</action>
      <action>Add to resolution tracking list: {{resolved_review_items}}</action>

      <!-- Mark task in Review Follow-ups section -->
      <action>Mark task checkbox [x] in "Tasks/Subtasks → Review Follow-ups (AI)" section</action>

      <!-- CRITICAL: Also mark corresponding action item in review section -->
      <action>Find matching action item in "Senior Developer Review (AI) → Action Items" section by matching description</action>
      <action>Mark that action item checkbox [x] as resolved</action>

      <action>Add to Dev Agent Record → Completion Notes: "✅ Resolved review finding [{{severity}}]: {{description}}"</action>
    </check>

    <!-- ONLY MARK COMPLETE IF ALL VALIDATION PASS -->
    <check if="ALL validation gates pass AND tests ACTUALLY exist and pass">
      <action>ONLY THEN mark the task (and subtasks) checkbox with [x]</action>
      <action>Update File List section with ALL new, modified, or deleted files (paths relative to repo root)</action>
      <action>Add completion notes to Dev Agent Record summarizing what was ACTUALLY implemented and tested</action>
    </check>

    <check if="ANY validation fails">
      <action>DO NOT mark task complete - fix issues first</action>
      <action>HALT if unable to fix validation failures</action>
    </check>

    <check if="review_continuation == true and {{resolved_review_items}} is not empty">
      <action>Count total resolved review items in this session</action>
      <action>Add Change Log entry: "Addressed code review findings - {{resolved_count}} items resolved (Date: {{date}})"</action>
    </check>

    <action>Save the story file</action>
    <action>Determine if more incomplete tasks remain</action>
    <action if="more tasks remain">
      <goto step="5">Next task</goto>
    </action>
    <action if="no tasks remain">
      <goto step="9">Completion</goto>
    </action>
  </step>

  <step n="9" goal="Story completion and mark for review" tag="sprint-status">
    <action>Verify ALL tasks and subtasks are marked [x] (re-scan the story document now)</action>
    <action>Run the full regression suite (do not skip)</action>
    <action>Confirm File List includes every changed file</action>
    <action>Execute enhanced definition-of-done validation</action>
    <action>Update the story Status to: "review"</action>

    <!-- Enhanced Definition of Done Validation -->
    <action>Validate definition-of-done checklist with essential requirements:
      - All tasks/subtasks marked complete with [x]
      - Implementation satisfies every Acceptance Criterion
      - Unit tests for core functionality added/updated
      - Integration tests for component interactions added when required
      - End-to-end tests for critical flows added when story demands them
      - All tests pass (no regressions, new tests successful)
      - Code quality checks pass (linting, static analysis if configured)
      - File List includes every new/modified/deleted file (relative paths)
      - Dev Agent Record contains implementation notes
      - Change Log includes summary of changes
      - Only permitted story sections were modified
    </action>

    <!-- Mark story ready for review - sprint status conditional -->
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists AND {{current_sprint_status}} != 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}</action>
      <action>Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}</action>
      <action>Verify current status is "in-progress" (expected previous state)</action>
      <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "review"</action>
      <action>Update last_updated field to current date</action>
      <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure including STATUS DEFINITIONS</action>
      <output>✅ Story status updated to "review" in sprint-status.yaml</output>
    </check>

    <check if="{sprint_status} file does NOT exist OR {{current_sprint_status}} == 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <output>ℹ️ Story status updated to "review" in story file (no sprint tracking configured)</output>
    </check>

    <check if="story key not found in sprint status">
      <output>⚠️ Story file updated, but sprint-status update failed: {{story_key}} not found

        Story status is set to "review" in file, but sprint-status.yaml may be out of sync.
      </output>
    </check>

    <!-- Final validation gates -->
    <action if="any task is incomplete">HALT - Complete remaining tasks before marking ready for review</action>
    <action if="regression failures exist">HALT - Fix regression issues before completing</action>
    <action if="File List is incomplete">HALT - Update File List with all changed files</action>
    <action if="definition-of-done validation fails">HALT - Address DoD failures before completing</action>
  </step>

  <step n="10" goal="Completion communication and user support">
    <action>Execute the enhanced definition-of-done checklist using the validation framework</action>
    <action>Prepare a concise summary in Dev Agent Record → Completion Notes</action>

    <action>Communicate to {user_name} that story implementation is complete and ready for review</action>
    <action>Summarize key accomplishments: story ID, story key, title, key changes made, tests added, files modified</action>
    <action>Provide the story file path and current status (now "review")</action>

    <action>Based on {user_skill_level}, ask if user needs any explanations about:
      - What was implemented and how it works
      - Why certain technical decisions were made
      - How to test or verify the changes
      - Any patterns, libraries, or approaches used
      - Anything else they'd like clarified
    </action>

    <check if="user asks for explanations">
      <action>Provide clear, contextual explanations tailored to {user_skill_level}</action>
      <action>Use examples and references to specific code when helpful</action>
    </check>

    <action>Once explanations are complete (or user indicates no questions), suggest logical next steps</action>
    <action>Recommended next steps (flexible based on project setup):
      - Review the implemented story and test the changes
      - Verify all acceptance criteria are met
      - Ensure deployment readiness if applicable
      - Run `code-review` workflow for peer review
      - Optional: If Test Architect module installed, run `/bmad:tea:automate` to expand guardrail tests
    </action>

    <output>💡 **Tip:** For best results, run `code-review` using a **different** LLM than the one that implemented this story.</output>
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists">
      <action>Suggest checking {sprint_status} to see project progress</action>
    </check>
    <action>Remain flexible - allow user to choose their own path or ask for other assistance</action>
  </step>

</workflow>

# generated: 2026-04-20
# last_updated: 2026-04-23 (5-1 backlog → ready-for-dev; epic-5 backlog → in-progress via create-story)
# project: bmad-module-skill-forge
# project_key: NOKEY
# tracking_system: file-system
# story_location: {project-root}/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts

# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:

1	# Story 6.2: Remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`
2	
3	Status: ready-for-dev
4	
5	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
6	
7	> **Precondition summary (2026-04-24 create-story time):** Epic 5 is `done` (v1.0.0 live on npm under `--tag latest` with SLSA L2 provenance since `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`). Story 6.1 is `done` — PR #222 merged at commit [`309f558`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/309f558) on `2026-04-24T12:55:35Z`; `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` and `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` are physically gone from `main` (verified via `curl -sI` returning `HTTP/2 404` and `gh workflow list` omitting `Publish to NPM` / `Manual Release`). `main` tip is `309f558` — the legacy-workflow cleanup commit. Sprint-status shows `epic-6: in-progress`, `6-1-…: done`, `6-2-…: backlog → ready-for-dev` (flipping on this create-story call), `6-3-…: backlog`, `epic-6-retrospective: optional`.
8	>
9	> **What Story 6.2 is:** a surgical `package.json` cleanup under FR19 (clause 2 of 3). The five `release:*`-shape script keys in `package.json:48-52` (`release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`) have been inert `[DEPRECATED]` fail-loud stubs since Story 4.2 (`echo >&2 && exit 1` prefix). With Story 6.1 having removed the legacy workflows they were warning about, the warning bullet in `CONTRIBUTING.md:85` and the stub scripts themselves are now stale-by-construction and can be removed outright. Two files touched in one commit: the `package.json` script removals and the `CONTRIBUTING.md` bullet prune.
10	>
11	> **Relationship to Epic 6 siblings.** Story 6.1 shipped first (workflow deletion, done). Story 6.2 ships this PR (script deletion). Story 6.3 (remove `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret) follows. **No bundling** per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 5 — distinct risk profiles (workflow deletion / script deletion / secret deletion) mean separate PRs = separate reverts = smaller rollback surface. Epic 5 retro Commitment 3 additionally warns: Story 6.2 MUST NOT bundle issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility) — that is a separate install-UX defect in separate territory.
12	
13	## Story
14	
15	As the maintainer,
16	I want the five deprecated `release:*` script keys physically removed from `package.json` and the now-stale warning bullet pruned from `CONTRIBUTING.md`,
17	so that the npm script surface reflects the actual supported operations, no operator is tempted to bypass the canonical `.github/workflows/release.yaml` CI path by invoking a local `npm run release:patch`, and the repo's release-retirement arc (Story 6.1 deleted the legacy workflows; this story deletes the legacy npm script entrypoints) advances one more step toward the single-rooted OIDC-only surface Story 6.3 will close.
18	
19	## Acceptance Criteria
20	
21	1. **Scope envelope — one commit, two files touched.** Subject: `chore(release): remove release:* scripts from package.json (Story 6.2)`. Touches EXACTLY two files:
22	   - `package.json` (MODIFIED — five `release:*` script keys deleted from the `scripts` block; AC 4)
23	   - `CONTRIBUTING.md` (MODIFIED — the `Do not use npm run release:* locally` bullet at `:85` deleted; AC 5)
24	
25	   **Verification:** `git show --stat HEAD` on the merge commit returns exactly those two files with `M` status; `git log main --grep='Story 6.2' --oneline` returns exactly the commit subject above; `git diff main~1..main --name-only` enumerates exactly `CONTRIBUTING.md` + `package.json` with zero additional paths.
26	
27	2. **Epic 6 precondition verification — required before branch open.** Per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 1 pattern (live-recon at Task 1, not trust-the-spec), the dev agent SHALL verify on `origin/main` at Task 1 time that every one of the following is true, and record the recon transcript verbatim in Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References. If any precondition fails, HALT — Story 6.2 cannot ship without Story 6.1's deletion confirmed:
28	   - (a) `git log main --grep='Story 6.1' --oneline` → one commit hit; `git rev-parse origin/main` returns `309f558…` or a later SHA
29	   - (b) `curl -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/main/.github/workflows/publish.yaml` → `HTTP/2 404` (confirms Story 6.1 actually shipped)
30	   - (c) `curl -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/main/.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` → `HTTP/2 404` (ditto)
31	   - (d) `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/package.json --jq '.name'` → `package.json` (confirms file present on remote; deletion target reachable)
32	   - (e) `jq -r '.scripts | keys | map(select(startswith("release"))) | .[]' package.json` SHALL list exactly five keys: `release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`. If any additional `release:*` key is present (e.g., `release:alpha`, `release:beta`), STOP and refresh AC 4 enumeration before proceeding — the epic's example list was non-exhaustive (line 750 "e.g., `release:patch`, `release:minor`, `release:major`, `release:alpha`, `release:beta`") but the authoritative set is whatever currently exists in `package.json` at Task 1 time.
33	   - (f) `grep -c 'npm run release:\*\|release:\* script' CONTRIBUTING.md` → at least `1` (confirms the AC 5 target bullet is present and matches expected shape)
34	   - (g) `grep -rn 'release:patch\|release:minor\|release:major\|release:prerelease\|npm run release' --include='*.md' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.yml' --include='*.json' . | grep -v 'node_modules\|package-lock\|CHANGELOG\.md\|_bmad-output/\|release-audits/'` — cross-check against AC 4 + AC 5 planned edits. Any hit not enumerated in this story's scope is a ripple the spec missed; record it and decide whether to include-in-scope (if trivially stale) or defer-with-issue-comment (if it lives in doc territory this story isn't authorized to edit).
35	
36	3. **PR description requirements.** The PR body SHALL include verbatim:
37	   - (a) An explicit reference to FR19 — e.g., `Advances FR19 "delete publish.yaml/manual-release.yaml (done in Story 6.1); remove release:* (this story); remove NPM_TOKEN secret (Story 6.3)"`. Story 6.2 satisfies the second clause.
38	   - (b) The enabling-precondition citation: Story 6.1 PR #222 merge commit `309f558` + `gh workflow list` output showing `Publish to NPM` / `Manual Release` absent. Story 6.1's deletion means the `[DEPRECATED]` stub scripts in `package.json` no longer have a canonical-path workflow pointer target from an operator's perspective — the stubs were warning against invoking workflows that no longer exist, so the warnings themselves are now vacuous.
39	   - (c) A two-file touched-list with one-line per-file purpose (matching AC 1's M/M breakdown).
40	   - (d) An explicit NOT-bundled statement: "Does not touch `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret (Story 6.3's territory). Does not touch `package.json.main` field (Epic 5 retro P7 standing flag; declined scope creep per Commitment 5). Does not touch `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:123` transcript (historical record; same preservation principle as Story 6.1 AC 7(d) for `docs/RELEASING.md:431`). Does not bundle issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility) per Epic 5 retro Commitment 3."
41	   - (e) NO `Fixes #NN` trailer (no same-repo issue tracks this story; FR19 reference form is sufficient). NO `_bmad-output/todo/` references (per user-memory `feedback_no_issue_numbers_in_pr.md`). Cite Story ACs in `AC N` form (not `#N`).
42	
43	4. **`package.json` scripts block — delete five keys.** The following five entries SHALL be removed from the `scripts` object in `package.json`:
44	   - `"release"` (currently `package.json:48`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] Use .github/workflows/release.yaml via GitHub Actions. See docs/RELEASING.md.' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm run release:patch`)
45	   - `"release:major"` (currently `:49`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version major && git push --follow-tags`)
46	   - `"release:minor"` (currently `:50`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version minor && git push --follow-tags`)
47	   - `"release:patch"` (currently `:51`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version patch && git push --follow-tags`)
48	   - `"release:prerelease"` (currently `:52`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version prerelease && git push --follow-tags`)
49	
50	   **Edit strategy:** delete the five lines outright. Do NOT add a tombstone comment — JSON doesn't support comments, and the git commit body already documents the removal. Preserve exact key ordering of the remaining scripts (the file is sorted by `prettier-plugin-packagejson`; `prettier --write` on post-edit `package.json` SHALL produce zero diff — confirmed by the AC 8 `format:check` gate).
51	
52	   **Verification:** post-edit `jq -r '.scripts | keys | map(select(startswith("release"))) | length' package.json` → `0`; `jq -r '.scripts | keys | length' package.json` returns the prior count minus 5 (pre-edit count is 32 scripts, confirmed by `jq -r '.scripts | keys | length' package.json` at create-story recon time; post-edit expected 27).
53	
54	5. **`CONTRIBUTING.md` edit — prune stale bullet.** The bullet at `CONTRIBUTING.md:85` reading:
55	
56	   > `- **Do not use \`npm run release:*\` locally.** Every \`release:*\` script (plus the bare \`release\` alias) is retained only as a fail-loud stub: it prints a \`[DEPRECATED]\` warning pointing at the canonical path and exits 1. The stubs exist to catch muscle-memory \`npm run release\` invocations before they ship anything; a post-v1.0.0 cleanup pass removes them outright.`
57	
58	   SHALL be deleted outright. No replacement bullet is added — once the scripts are gone, "don't invoke them" is vacuously satisfied (same principle as Story 6.1 AC 6 for the legacy-workflow bullet). The surrounding `## Releasing` section's other prose — the canonical-path bullet at `:84` and the `See [docs/RELEASING.md]` paragraph at `:87` — remain unchanged. The final post-v1.0.0 cleanup pass the bullet's prose promised is literally this story; the prose becomes self-contradictory after the deletion, which is why it must also be pruned.
59	
60	   **Verification:** post-edit `grep -n 'release:\*\|release alias\|fail-loud stub\|muscle-memory' CONTRIBUTING.md` returns zero hits; the `## Releasing` section's line count drops by ~1 (the bullet is a single long line). Re-read the `## Releasing` section end-to-end post-edit to confirm the remaining two paragraphs flow naturally without the removed bullet.
61	
62	6. **Scope exclusions — three items explicitly declined.**
63	   - (a) **`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:123`** contains a verbatim transcript of the `release:patch` stub body as evidence of the Story 4.2 `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence audit. This is past-tense historical record, not a current-state claim, and preserves the audit lineage (same principle as Story 6.1 AC 7(d) preserving the `docs/RELEASING.md:431` `Story 3.3 Patch A` reference). Do NOT prune.
64	   - (b) **`package.json` `main` field** points at `tools/cli/skf-cli.js` (side-effectful CLI entrypoint). Epic 5 retro P7 flagged this as "Addressable in Epic 6 if `package.json` is being touched for Story 6-2 anyway." The retro's own Commitment 5 counsel ("distinct risk profiles = distinct reverts = smaller rollback surface") overrides P7's soft suggestion here: a `main`-field change would couple a Node-API-surface decision into a script-removal revert, expanding blast radius. Declined; tracked as standing flag, future story territory. If the user disagrees at code-review time, a separate follow-up PR is the right shape.
65	   - (c) **Issue #211** (piped-stdin install infeasibility) is tangentially related (Story 5.2 triaged it after the install UI couldn't be driven by piped stdin in the smoke test), but it tracks a separate install-UX defect in the CLI multiselect, not a `release:*` script behavior. Per Epic 5 retro Commitment 3, MUST NOT bundle into Story 6.2.
66	
67	   Verification is conversational (the PR description's AC 3(d) NOT-bundled statement + the Dev Notes rationale). No CLI check required.
68	
69	7. **`npm run` invocation produces `Missing script` after merge.** Per the Epic 6 acceptance statement: "When a user runs `npm run release:patch` (or any legacy invocation) Then npm SHALL report 'Missing script: release:patch' — confirming removal." The dev agent SHALL verify post-merge on local `main` (fast-forwarded past the merge commit) by running:
70	
71	   ```bash
72	   for key in release release:major release:minor release:patch release:prerelease; do
73	     npm run "$key" 2>&1 | tail -2
74	   done
75	   ```
76	
77	   Each invocation SHALL print `npm error Missing script: "$key"` (exit code non-zero) — confirming npm no longer resolves the key. Record the five-invocation transcript verbatim in Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References as AC 7 proof.
78	
79	8. **Quality gate (`npm run quality`) passes before PR open.** All 13 subcommands SHALL exit 0. Main failure vectors to watch (informed by Epic 5 retro Commitment 9 adversarial review pattern):
80	   - `format:check` (Prettier on `package.json`): the file is `prettier-plugin-packagejson`-sorted; post-edit run MUST produce zero diff. If `prettier --write` changes key ordering or spacing, the edit's whitespace is wrong — fix before commit, do NOT `--no-verify`.
81	   - `lint` / `lint:fix` on `package.json`: ESLint's `.yaml` extension isn't touched here; `package.json` is only covered by `prettier-plugin-packagejson` ordering + `lint-staged` `prettier --write`.
82	   - `lint:md` on `CONTRIBUTING.md`: verify balanced Markdown — bullet-list breakage is the main risk after deleting a multi-line `-` entry. Run `markdownlint-cli2 CONTRIBUTING.md` standalone first if uncertain; MD031 (fenced-code blank-line) and MD032 (list blank-line) are the usual suspects when mid-bullet deletions leave stray blank lines.
83	   - `test` / `test:*`: no test depends on `release:*` scripts (verified pre-edit via `grep -rn 'release:patch\|release:minor\|release:major\|release:prerelease\|"release"' test/` returns zero hits). Post-edit `npm test` SHALL pass unchanged — AC 8 sentinel.
84	   - `validate:refs` / `validate:schemas` / `validate:skills`: orthogonal; `tools/validate-file-refs.js` scans `src/` only, `package.json` editing doesn't trip them.
85	   - `docs:validate-drift` / `docs:validate-links`: orthogonal; `CONTRIBUTING.md` is not part of the docs-site content tree (`website/` + `docs/` are).
86	
87	9. **Lockfile invariance.** `package-lock.json` SHALL remain byte-identical pre- and post-edit. **Verification:** `sha256sum package-lock.json` before and after the edit commit SHALL match exactly. **Rationale:** the five removed entries are `scripts` keys, not `dependencies` / `devDependencies` entries; npm's lockfile doesn't track script definitions. If the lockfile changes (e.g., because the dev agent accidentally ran `npm install` with a dirty `package.json`), that indicates another `package.json` drift unrelated to this story — investigate and separate before commit.
88	
89	10. **Branch-protection compliance — PR only, not direct push.** Per Story 1.1 branch protection ruleset `Default`, direct push to `main` is blocked for all actors. The commit SHALL be authored on a fresh branch `feat/v1-cleanup-release-scripts` from `origin/main` at the Story 6.1 merge tip (currently `309f558`). The PR SHALL be gated by the 7 required status checks (eslint, markdownlint, prettier, validate ubuntu+windows, python ubuntu+windows). Self-approve OR admin-bypass-merge — match the Story 5.3 / 5.4 / 6.1 pattern (single-maintainer solo-approval per Story 1.2 bypass-list entry).

92	    - (a) `git show HEAD -- package.json` post-merge SHALL show exactly five `-` lines (the five deleted script entries) and zero `+` lines in the `scripts` block.
93	    - (b) `git show HEAD -- CONTRIBUTING.md` post-merge SHALL show exactly one `-` line (the deleted bullet).
94	    - (c) Local `main` fast-forwarded through the merge commit; re-run AC 7's `npm run release:*` loop — all five SHALL report `Missing script`.

Record both as supporting evidence for the "npm registry shows historical; repo main shows current" lineage.

98	    - Subject: `chore(release): remove release:* scripts from package.json (Story 6.2)` (68 chars — under the conventional-commits 72-char soft limit; Story 6.1's 88-char subject was the outlier, this returns to the sub-72 convention used in Epic 2 / 3 / 4 stories).
99	    - Body: the PR description content per AC 3 (FR19 reference, Story 6.1 merge-evidence, two-file list, NOT-bundled statement, AC 13 self-reference).
100	    - NO `Fixes #NN` trailer. NO `_bmad-output/todo/` references. Cite story ACs as `AC N` (not `#N`).

106	    - `6-2-remove-release-scripts-from-package-json: ready-for-dev → in-progress` after the dev agent claims the work (Task 2 start).

No `release` GitHub Environment config change. No `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret change (Story 6-3's territory). No dependency addition/removal (lockfile invariance — AC 9). 149	  - [ ] AC 11(b): `git show HEAD -- CONTRIBUTING.md` — SHALL show exactly one `-` line (the deleted bullet; Markdown bullets are single-line even when wrapped in rendered output — the underlying source is one `\n`-terminated line)

168	## Dev Notes
169	
170	### What this story actually is
171	
172	A **surgical two-file cleanup** covering one clause of FR19. Delete the five `release:*`-shape script keys from `package.json` (`release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`) and prune the now-stale `Do not use npm run release:*` bullet at `CONTRIBUTING.md:85`. Single commit, single PR, two files touched. The deletion is safe NOW because Story 6.1 (PR #222, merged `309f558` on `2026-04-24T12:55:35Z`) removed the legacy workflows the stubs were warning against — the deprecation message ("Use `.github/workflows/release.yaml`") remains valid (that's still the canonical path), but the muscle-memory-catch value proposition evaporates when the legacy targets are gone. The stubs now warn against a non-existent alternative; the bullet says a cleanup pass "removes them outright"; the logical closure of both is deletion.
173	
174	**What this story is NOT:**
175	
176	- NOT a release. Zero `release.yaml` dispatch, zero `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch, zero npm state change. NFR6 v1.0.0 immutability is vacuously respected (the registry's published v1.0.0 `package.json` is byte-frozen on npm and cannot be affected by repo edits).
177	- NOT a `docs/RELEASING.md` edit. Pre-edit recon (AC 2(g)) confirms `docs/RELEASING.md` contains zero references to `release:*` scripts — Story 4.1's rollback playbook never documented them as a recovery path (it points only to `release.yaml` GitHub Actions dispatch). Epic 6 acceptance criterion (c) ("`docs/RELEASING.md` SHALL remain the canonical reference and SHALL describe only the `release.yaml`-via-GitHub-Actions path") is satisfied by Story 4.1 + current state, not by this story's edits — it's a verification condition, not an edit directive.
178	- NOT a README.md edit. Recon confirms README has no `release:*` references either.
179	- NOT a `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` edit. The `:123` transcript of the `release:patch` stub body is historical audit evidence (Story 4.2 `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence record) — same preservation principle as Story 6.1 AC 7(d) preserving `docs/RELEASING.md:431`'s Story 3.3 Patch A historical reference. Do NOT prune.
180	- NOT an `NPM_TOKEN` secret removal. Story 6-3's territory. The secret stays untouched through this story.
181	- NOT a `package.json.main` field change. Epic 5 retro P7 soft-suggested this as "addressable if `package.json` is being touched for 6-2 anyway"; Epic 5 retro Commitment 5's no-bundling discipline overrides that soft suggestion here (distinct risk profiles = distinct reverts). Declined; tracked as standing flag for a future story.
182	- NOT an issue #211 fix. Epic 5 retro Commitment 3 explicitly forbids bundling. The piped-stdin install infeasibility is a CLI-UX defect in separate territory.
183	
184	### Load-bearing decisions already made at create-story (no Dev-Agent re-derivation needed)
185	
186	**(A) Single-commit envelope, not two.** The five-scripts + one-bullet edit is atomic — splitting the script deletion from the bullet prune would land `main` in a transient state where the scripts are gone but `CONTRIBUTING.md` still warns against them (reader confusion). One commit, one PR, one revert surface. Same reasoning as Story 6.1 Dev Notes (A).
187	
188	**(B) Five keys, not "release:alpha/beta + whatever else the epic mentioned".** The epic's AC enumeration (`e.g., release:patch, release:minor, release:major, release:alpha, release:beta`) is non-exhaustive — the `e.g.` admits the list isn't canonical. The authoritative set is whatever exists in `package.json` at Task 1 time. Pre-edit recon (AC 2(e)) confirms five: `release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`. `release:alpha` and `release:beta` do NOT exist in this repo (nor did they per `git log --all -- package.json | grep -i 'release:alpha\|release:beta'` returning nothing). The spec defensively widens the recon check to catch any unknown `release:*` that may have been added post-retro (zero probability, but the check costs nothing).
189	
190	**(C) `CONTRIBUTING.md` bullet is deleted, not rewritten.** The bullet's sole purpose is to warn against muscle-memory invocation of soon-to-be-removed stubs. Once the stubs ARE removed, the warning is vacuous — invoking a nonexistent script produces an unambiguous `Missing script` error, which is a louder warning than any prose bullet. Adding a replacement bullet like "these scripts have been removed; use `.github/workflows/release.yaml`" would be CHANGELOG-style narration that belongs in git history, not in a maintainer onboarding doc. Same principle as Story 6.1 Dev Notes (C): retire the deprecation warning when the deprecated thing is retired.
191	
192	**(D) No replacement bullet even if the `## Releasing` section looks thin post-edit.** Post-edit, the section has: (1) canonical-path bullet pointing at `release.yaml` + GitHub Actions, (2) `See docs/RELEASING.md` paragraph. That is sufficient for a contributor's onboarding need — the two bullets say "use this" + "read the full procedure here." Adding a third bullet about "scripts removed" would be retroactive narration; git log is the audit log, not `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
193	
194	**(E) `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:123` historical reference stays.** The transcript shows the Story 4.2 stub body with `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr routing — it is evidence that the audit at v1.0.0 launch time recorded a conscious divergence decision. Deleting it would erase the audit lineage. The same principle applies as Story 6.1 AC 7(d) for `docs/RELEASING.md:431`. Audit artifacts are point-in-time records; they don't follow repo state forward.
195	
196	**(F) `package.json.main` field stays untouched even though the file is being touched.** Epic 5 retro P7 argued "addressable if package.json is being touched for 6-2 anyway" — this seems seductive but the costs are: (1) a `main`-field change couples the script-removal revert surface with an API-surface decision surface (two distinct operational concerns, per Commitment 5 the exact anti-pattern Epic 5 retro flagged); (2) P7's own framing marks it a "standing flag," which is retro-housekeeping territory, not current-story territory; (3) STABILITY.md Path B already handles the semantic concern by declaring no programmatic API. Decline here; if the user wants to bundle it, they can elect that at code-review time — the rejected-by-default stance keeps the commit's scope claim honest.
197	
198	**(G) Branch name `feat/v1-cleanup-release-scripts` follows Story 5.3/5.4/6.1 convention.** Prefix `feat/v1-` keeps the v1-era cleanup branches grouped; `cleanup-release-scripts` is specific enough to distinguish from Story 6-1's `feat/v1-cleanup-legacy-workflows` and the forthcoming Story 6-3's `feat/v1-cleanup-npm-token`. Do NOT reuse any Epic 5 branch or Story 6-1's branch.
199	
200	**(H) Lockfile invariance is load-bearing, not merely expected.** Epic 5 retro's Story 2.1 post-mortem noted that a silent `packages[""].devDependencies.zod` mirror-entry drift had been re-added during an unrelated install. Any `npm install` run in this story's dev agent shell could trip the same class of defect on `package.json`'s 24 devDependencies. AC 9's `sha256sum` belt-and-braces check catches it. Rationale: the five removed entries are `scripts` keys, not `dependencies` / `devDependencies` — npm's lockfile does not track scripts, so the lockfile SHOULD be byte-identical. If it isn't, something else changed.
201	
202	**(I) Prettier's `prettier-plugin-packagejson` sort order matters.** The plugin sorts the `scripts` object alphabetically by key (observable in `package.json:35-67`). Deleting five keys should not perturb sort order of the remaining 23; `prettier --check` via the `format:check` subcommand is the enforcement gate. If the dev agent's manual edit disturbs whitespace or sort order, `format:check` catches it before commit — do NOT `--no-verify` the hook; fix the edit.

Clean closure of the Story 4.2 → Story 6.2 arc.

That published tarball is untouched by this repo edit — the registry-vs-repo divergence is expected and correct (the registry is the historical ground truth for what v1.0.0 shipped; the repo's current `package.json` is the source of truth for what v1.0.1+ will ship).

# Story 6.2: Remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

> **Precondition summary (2026-04-24 create-story time):** Epic 5 is `done` (v1.0.0 live on npm under `--tag latest` with SLSA L2 provenance since `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`). Story 6.1 is `done` — PR #222 merged at commit [`309f558`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/309f558) on `2026-04-24T12:55:35Z`; `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` and `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` are physically gone from `main` (verified via `curl -sI` returning `HTTP/2 404` and `gh workflow list` omitting `Publish to NPM` / `Manual Release`). `main` tip is `309f558` — the legacy-workflow cleanup commit. Sprint-status shows `epic-6: in-progress`, `6-1-…: done`, `6-2-…: backlog → ready-for-dev` (flipping on this create-story call), `6-3-…: backlog`, `epic-6-retrospective: optional`.
>
> **What Story 6.2 is:** a surgical `package.json` cleanup under FR19 (clause 2 of 3). The five `release:*`-shape script keys in `package.json:48-52` (`release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`) have been inert `[DEPRECATED]` fail-loud stubs since Story 4.2 (`echo >&2 && exit 1` prefix). With Story 6.1 having removed the legacy workflows they were warning about, the warning bullet in `CONTRIBUTING.md:85` and the stub scripts themselves are now stale-by-construction and can be removed outright. Two files touched in one commit: the `package.json` script removals and the `CONTRIBUTING.md` bullet prune.
>
> **Relationship to Epic 6 siblings.** Story 6.1 shipped first (workflow deletion, done). Story 6.2 ships this PR (script deletion). Story 6.3 (remove `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret) follows. **No bundling** per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 5 — distinct risk profiles (workflow deletion / script deletion / secret deletion) mean separate PRs = separate reverts = smaller rollback surface. Epic 5 retro Commitment 3 additionally warns: Story 6.2 MUST NOT bundle issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility) — that is a separate install-UX defect in separate territory.

## Story

As the maintainer,
I want the five deprecated `release:*` script keys physically removed from `package.json` and the now-stale warning bullet pruned from `CONTRIBUTING.md`,
so that the npm script surface reflects the actual supported operations, no operator is tempted to bypass the canonical `.github/workflows/release.yaml` CI path by invoking a local `npm run release:patch`, and the repo's release-retirement arc (Story 6.1 deleted the legacy workflows; this story deletes the legacy npm script entrypoints) advances one more step toward the single-rooted OIDC-only surface Story 6.3 will close.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Scope envelope — one commit, two files touched.** Subject: `chore(release): remove release:* scripts from package.json (Story 6.2)`. Touches EXACTLY two files:
   - `package.json` (MODIFIED — five `release:*` script keys deleted from the `scripts` block; AC 4)
   - `CONTRIBUTING.md` (MODIFIED — the `Do not use npm run release:* locally` bullet at `:85` deleted; AC 5)

   **Verification:** `git show --stat HEAD` on the merge commit returns exactly those two files with `M` status; `git log main --grep='Story 6.2' --oneline` returns exactly the commit subject above; `git diff main~1..main --name-only` enumerates exactly `CONTRIBUTING.md` + `package.json` with zero additional paths.

2. **Epic 6 precondition verification — required before branch open.** Per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 1 pattern (live-recon at Task 1, not trust-the-spec), the dev agent SHALL verify on `origin/main` at Task 1 time that every one of the following is true, and record the recon transcript verbatim in Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References. If any precondition fails, HALT — Story 6.2 cannot ship without Story 6.1's deletion confirmed:
   - (a) `git log main --grep='Story 6.1' --oneline` → one commit hit; `git rev-parse origin/main` returns `309f558…` or a later SHA
   - (b) `curl -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/main/.github/workflows/publish.yaml` → `HTTP/2 404` (confirms Story 6.1 actually shipped)
   - (c) `curl -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/main/.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` → `HTTP/2 404` (ditto)

## Dev Notes

### What this story actually is

A **surgical two-file cleanup** covering one clause of FR19. Delete the five `release:*`-shape script keys from `package.json` (`release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`) and prune the now-stale `Do not use npm run release:*` bullet at `CONTRIBUTING.md:85`. Single commit, single PR, two files touched. The deletion is safe NOW because Story 6.1 (PR #222, merged `309f558` on `2026-04-24T12:55:35Z`) removed the legacy workflows the stubs were warning against — the deprecation message ("Use `.github/workflows/release.yaml`") remains valid (that's still the canonical path), but the muscle-memory-catch value proposition evaporates when the legacy targets are gone. The stubs now warn against a non-existent alternative; the bullet says a cleanup pass "removes them outright"; the logical closure of both is deletion.

**What this story is NOT:**

- NOT a release. Zero `release.yaml` dispatch, zero `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch, zero npm state change. NFR6 v1.0.0 immutability is vacuously respected (the registry's published v1.0.0 `package.json` is byte-frozen on npm and cannot be affected by repo edits).
- NOT a `docs/RELEASING.md` edit. Pre-edit recon (AC 2(g)) confirms `docs/RELEASING.md` contains zero references to `release:*` scripts — Story 4.1's rollback playbook never documented them as a recovery path (it points only to `release.yaml` GitHub Actions dispatch). Epic 6 acceptance criterion (c) ("`docs/RELEASING.md` SHALL remain the canonical reference and SHALL describe only the `release.yaml`-via-GitHub-Actions path") is satisfied by Story 4.1 + current state, not by this story's edits — it's a verification condition, not an edit directive.
- NOT a README.md edit. Recon confirms README has no `release:*` references either.
- NOT a `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` edit. The `:123` transcript of the `release:patch` stub body is historical audit evidence (Story 4.2 `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence record) — same preservation principle as Story 6.1 AC 7(d) preserving `docs/RELEASING.md:431`'s Story 3.3 Patch A historical reference. Do NOT prune.
- NOT an `NPM_TOKEN` secret removal. Story 6-3's territory. The secret stays untouched through this story.
- NOT a `package.json.main` field change. Epic 5 retro P7 soft-suggested this as "addressable if `package.json` is being touched for 6-2 anyway"; Epic 5 retro Commitment 5's no-bundling discipline overrides that soft suggestion here (distinct risk profiles = distinct reverts). Declined; tracked as standing flag for a future story.
- NOT an issue #211 fix. Epic 5 retro Commitment 3 explicitly forbids bundling. The piped-stdin install infeasibility is a CLI-UX defect in separate territory.

### Load-bearing decisions already made at create-story (no Dev-Agent re-derivation needed)

**(A) Single-commit envelope, not two.** The five-scripts + one-bullet edit is atomic — splitting the script deletion from the bullet prune would land `main` in a transient state where the scripts are gone but `CONTRIBUTING.md` still warns against them (reader confusion). One commit, one PR, one revert surface. Same reasoning as Story 6.1 Dev Notes (A).

**(B) Five keys, not "release:alpha/beta + whatever else the epic mentioned".** The epic's AC enumeration (`e.g., release:patch, release:minor, release:major, release:alpha, release:beta`) is non-exhaustive — the `e.g.` admits the list isn't canonical. The authoritative set is whatever exists in `package.json` at Task 1 time. Pre-edit recon (AC 2(e)) confirms five: `release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`. `release:alpha` and `release:beta` do NOT exist in this repo (nor did they per `git log --all -- package.json | grep -i 'release:alpha\|release:beta'` returning nothing). The spec defensively widens the recon check to catch any unknown `release:*` that may have been added post-retro (zero probability, but the check costs nothing).

**(C) `CONTRIBUTING.md` bullet is deleted, not rewritten.** The bullet's sole purpose is to warn against muscle-memory invocation of soon-to-be-removed stubs. Once the stubs ARE removed, the warning is vacuous — invoking a nonexistent script produces an unambiguous `Missing script` error, which is a louder warning than any prose bullet. Adding a replacement bullet like "these scripts have been removed; use `.github/workflows/release.yaml`" would be CHANGELOG-style narration that belongs in git history, not in a maintainer onboarding doc. Same principle as Story 6.1 Dev Notes (C): retire the deprecation warning when the deprecated thing is retired.

**(D) No replacement bullet even if the `## Releasing` section looks thin post-edit.** Post-edit, the section has: (1) canonical-path bullet pointing at `release.yaml` + GitHub Actions, (2) `See docs/RELEASING.md` paragraph. That is sufficient for a contributor's onboarding need — the two bullets say "use this" + "read the full procedure here." Adding a third bullet about "scripts removed" would be retroactive narration; git log is the audit log, not `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

**(E) `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:123` historical reference stays.** The transcript shows the Story 4.2 stub body with `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr routing — it is evidence that the audit at v1.0.0 launch time recorded a conscious divergence decision. Deleting it would erase the audit lineage. The same principle applies as Story 6.1 AC 7(d) for `docs/RELEASING.md:431`. Audit artifacts are point-in-time records; they don't follow repo state forward.

**(F) `package.json.main` field stays untouched even though the file is being touched.** Epic 5 retro P7 argued "addressable if package.json is being touched for 6-2 anyway" — this seems seductive but the costs are: (1) a `main`-field change couples the script-removal revert surface with an API-surface decision surface (two distinct operational concerns, per Commitment 5 the exact anti-pattern Epic 5 retro flagged); (2) P7's own framing marks it a "standing flag," which is retro-housekeeping territory, not current-story territory; (3) STABILITY.md Path B already handles the semantic concern by declaring no programmatic API. Decline here; if the user wants to bundle it, they can elect that at code-review time — the rejected-by-default stance keeps the commit's scope claim honest.

**(G) Branch name `feat/v1-cleanup-release-scripts` follows Story 5.3/5.4/6.1 convention.** Prefix `feat/v1-` keeps the v1-era cleanup branches grouped; `cleanup-release-scripts` is specific enough to distinguish from Story 6-1's `feat/v1-cleanup-legacy-workflows` and the forthcoming Story 6-3's `feat/v1-cleanup-npm-token`. Do NOT reuse any Epic 5 branch or Story 6-1's branch.

**(H) Lockfile invariance is load-bearing, not merely expected.** Epic 5 retro's Story 2.1 post-mortem noted that a silent `packages[""].devDependencies.zod` mirror-entry drift had been re-added during an unrelated install. Any `npm install` run in this story's dev agent shell could trip the same class of defect on `package.json`'s 24 devDependencies. AC 9's `sha256sum` belt-and-braces check catches it. Rationale: the five removed entries are `scripts` keys, not `dependencies` / `devDependencies` — npm's lockfile does not track scripts, so the lockfile SHOULD be byte-identical. If it isn't, something else changed.

**(I) Prettier's `prettier-plugin-packagejson` sort order matters.** The plugin sorts the `scripts` object alphabetically by key (observable in `package.json:35-67`). Deleting five keys should not perturb sort order of the remaining 23; `prettier --check` via the `format:check` subcommand is the enforcement gate. If the dev agent's manual edit disturbs whitespace or sort order, `format:check` catches it before commit — do NOT `--no-verify` the hook; fix the edit.
